title: stylidium angustifolium
intro: Stylidium angustifolium, also known as Narrow-leaved Trigger Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. It is a small, annual herb native to Western Australia, characterized by its distinctive trigger-like mechanism that triggers a movement in response to touch. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stylidium angustifolium include soothing skin irritations and wounds, reducing fever, and acting as a natural antiseptic, and it can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures.
